text: This qualitative field study examines pedagogical practices used by teachers of Islamic Religious Education and Ethics (PAI and BP) at an elementary school in Jayapura, Indonesia, specifically focused on the development of faith through the Asmaul Husna (Names of Allah Most Beautiful). Whilst students are taught the four names of Allah set out in the National Curriculum, the PAI/BP teachers at the School of SDN 2 Kesumadadi teach the entire list of Asmaul Husna as a means to inculcate religious values, in order to create student’s long lasting spiritual character. The study collected its data through observation, longitudinal interviews and documentary analysis. The investigation indicates that the practices of reciting Asmaul Husna at the School of SDN 2 are carried out in the weekly religious classes at the school and therefore not carried out as an on going activity as a habit for students. Based on the collected data this study found that the character building at the School of SDN 2 is founded upon the reciting of Asmaul Husna practiced on a regular basis at the school and concluded that the teacher can develop students’ religious character through pedagogical practices as role models, through inculcating as an on-going habit of students’ activity, through a pro-active manner in mentoring students, by offering incentives to students, as well as achieving students’ devotion to have a self-controlled activity.
headline: Fully Human Written
